name: scheduled-agent-setup
summary: Created comprehensive calendar scheduler with CLI commands, enhanced recall_client.py with health-check functionality, and documented all scheduled agent specifications for V's review.

deliverables.description: calendar_scheduler.py - Core sync logic with CLI commands
features: status command to view scheduler state
schedule-event command (placeholder for specific event scheduling)
Video URL extraction (Zoom, Meet, Teams, WebEx, GoToMeeting)
health-check command to detect stuck/failed bots
Checks for bots stuck in in_call_recording state
Exits with non-zero if issues found

contents: Agent 1: ⇱ Recall Calendar Sync (15min business hours)
Agent 2: Recall Calendar Sync Off-hours (hourly overnight)
Agent 3: Recall Bot Health Check (30min intervals)
Agent ID storage template for N5/config/recall_agents.json
Rate limit considerations and safety margins

notes: Calendar events integration requires Zo's Google Calendar connection - scheduler provides placeholder fetch method
D2.1 (Calendar Scheduler Core) was not completed, so D2.2 created full calendar_scheduler.py
Agent specs are documented but NOT created - V must review and create manually
All CLI commands tested and working
Database models from D1.2 are used correctly
learnings: Recall API rate limit is 60 req/min - our 15-min sync cadence is well within safety margin
Minimum 10 minute lead time is required for scheduled bots - scheduler enforces this
health-check command exits non-zero on issues, enabling easy monitoring integration
next_steps: V creates scheduled agents using create_agent tool
V tests agents in sandbox mode before production
V stores agent IDs in N5/config/recall_agents.json
Monitor first few sync runs manually
